title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Type-System Trace: Dependency Lifetime Injection
summary: Middleware runs in an unsafe order, async rejections are wrapped twice, error handlers use the wrong signature, dependencies are created per request, and multiple layers write responses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts dependency lifetime injection increment in composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct long-lived dependencies at startup and inject narrow capabilities without request-time pools, mutable service locators, or hidden globals.
Implement four-argument error middleware, normalized failure taxonomy, disclosure policy, and headersSent delegation without double writes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Dependency Lifetime Injection · Theory Dependency Lifetime Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2230 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; record only bounded redacted diagnostic fields; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dependency Lifetime Injection contract for the supplied data.
